steps.title: Spot which variables differentiate the groups
steps.content:
Incubation duration and brood capacity create sharp contrasts between the groups—display species show 12–14 day incubation with 2–3 broods per year, while non-display species show 30–34 days with 1 brood per year.

The number of parental incubators, by contrast, appears equally in both groups (1 or 2 in each), showing no association with the display.

steps.title: Identify which conclusion matches this pattern
steps.content:
B correctly recognizes that incubation duration and brood capacity are more strongly associated with the display than the number of incubators is.

Choices A, C, and D fail by falsely claiming all three are equally associated, reversing the incubation pattern, or misreading the data.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
